channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says he's considering buying used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday he is considering buying used Boeing aircraft — perhaps from an overseas seller — to use as Air Force One when he's aboard, as he fumes over the U.S. plane-maker's delays in producing two specially modified ones for presidential use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Arkansas victims of mortuary theft scheme inspire push for law banning sale of human remains
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A year had passed since Doneysha Smith suffered a stillbirth and the memories of her loss lingered in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Agriculture Department tries to rehire fired workers tied to bird flu response
item.description: The Agriculture Department is scrambling to rehire several workers who were involved in the government's response to the ongoing bird flu outbreak that has devastated egg and poultry farms over the past three years.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KREX 5

item.title: Musk says he will check with Trump about DOGE stimulus checks
item.description: (KRON) -- Elon Musk said he will check with President Donald Trump about the possibility of sending $8,000 "DOGE stimulus" checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal government pulls approval for NYC congestion pricing
item.description: "New York State’s congestion pricing plan is a slap in the face to working class Americans and small business owners,” Secretary of Transportation Sean Duffy said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Cal Fire captain stabbed, killed in her home
item.description: A Cal Fire captain was fatally stabbed at her home in Ramona, California, Monday in what San Diego County Sheriff's Department officials are investigating as a suspected homicide.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KRON4

item.title: Jan. 6 pardon recipients argue it stretches to other crimes: report
item.description: Many defendants are arguing that crimes that were found during the riot investigations should be pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione speaks out for first time, sends message to supporters
item.description: "I am overwhelmed by - and grateful for - everyone who has written me to share their stories and express their support," Luigi Mangione wrote.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Bosar, NewsNation

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, KRON4

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Kelly Loeffler to lead Small Business Administration
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed former Sen. Kelly Loeffler (R-Ga.) to lead the Small Business Administration (SBA), keeping up a frantic pace to cement President Trump's team in place. Senators voted 52-46 on Loeffler's nomination.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KRON4

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: DHS says it 'can, should and will' administer polygraph exams amid ICE raid location leaks
item.description: DHS said it “can, should and will" administer polygraph exams to employees after Secretary Noem allegedly warned they will be used to combat potential leaks about upcoming ICE raids.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Native American activist Leonard Peltier, freed from prison, is welcomed on North Dakota reservation
item.description: Native American activist Leonard Peltier was defiant toward the government but grateful to his family and supporters as they welcomed him home to North Dakota, one day after his release from a Florida prison where he had been serving a life sentence in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Winter-weary East Coast hit with another storm as temperatures plunge elsewhere
item.description: Officials have urged residents to stay off the roads in portions of Virginia and North Carolina, where a storm dropped heavy snow and caused hundreds of accidents in places that aren’t accustomed to significant accumulations.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 4 takeaways from the Trump, Elon Musk interview
item.description: President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk sat down for a joint interview with Fox News’s Sean Hannity and defended the administration’s massive overhaul of the federal government while giving some insights into their friendship.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: JFK Presidential Library closes amid Trump federal worker culling
item.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum closed on Tuesday and will remain closed until further notice, a notice from its foundation read.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kohl's to close 27 underperforming stores by April 2025
item.description: Kohl's has announced the closure of 27 stores and the San Bernardino E-commerce Distribution center due to underperformance and the need to focus on long-term growth and health of the business.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, Fox 5

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9

item.title: NAACP urges boycott of companies that dumped DEI
item.description: (The Hill) - The NAACP is urging Black Americans to refrain from shopping at retailers that have stepped back from diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KTLA 5

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Top Russian, US officials discuss improving ties, ending the Ukraine war, without Kyiv
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The top diplomats from Russia and the U.S. met Tuesday in Saudi Arabia to discuss improving ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine — talks that represented a rapid and major change in American foreign policy under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, BARAA ANWER and DASHA LITVINOVA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, WGN 9

item.title: What is a polar vortex? US gets a taste of the Arctic this week
item.description: The term “polar vortex” usually refers to the gigantic circular upper air weather pattern in the Arctic region. It is a normal pattern that is stronger in the winter and keeps some of the coldest weather bottled up near the North Pole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration cuts reach FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration’s effort to slash the size of the federal workforce reached the Food and Drug Administration this weekend, as recently hired employees who review the safety of food ingredients, medical devices and other products were fired.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: MISSION, Texas (AP) — An idled Border Patrol bus sat empty this week, on standby for any migrants surrendering near the southern tip of Texas. Agents in two speedboats zipped past pockets of sandy shores, known landing spots for people entering the U.S.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
